title: Buffalo Chicken Wings
subtitle: Crispy oven-baked wings tossed in buttery hot sauce
sourceNotes: Buffalo wings were created at the Anchor Bar in Buffalo, New York in 1964 by Teressa Bellissimo. The original recipe used Frank's RedHot sauce and butter, the combination that remains the standard. The dish is documented in the New York Times (1980) and is now one of the most widely known American dishes internationally.

content.text: Dry wings crisp; wet wings steam. Pat them as dry as possible with kitchen paper before anything else. The baking powder toss is the oven version of a trick that draws out surface moisture during cooking. It gives a crispier skin than plain salt alone. Do not swap in bicarbonate of soda. It has a different effect.

Pat {{chicken-wings}} of wings completely dry. Toss with baking powder, salt, pepper, and garlic powder. Arrange on a wire rack over a baking sheet. Bake at 180 °C fan for 30 minutes, then increase to 220 °C fan and bake for 15 to 20 minutes more until deeply golden and crisp.
Melt {{butter-unsalted}} of butter and combine with {{hot-sauce}} of hot sauce. Add the garlic and honey if using. Toss the hot wings in the
immediately and serve with celery sticks and blue cheese dip or soured cream.
